source.name: Lane's Arabic-English Lexicon
source.author: Edward William Lane
source.license: The original work is public domain worldwide. IMPORTANT: the entries below are SAMPLE paraphrases of the classical meaning-range, NOT verbatim text from Lane's Lexicon. Replace with verified Lane's text (or OCR of the public-domain scans). If you reuse a digitised dataset (e.g. Perseus), honour that dataset's added licence (Perseus = CC BY-SA 3.0 US).
source.attribution: Edward William Lane, An Arabic-English Lexicon (London & Edinburgh: Williams & Norgate, 1863–1893).

senses.definitionEn: Mercy, compassion, tenderness — a pity or softness of heart that calls for the showing of kindness to its object.
senses.definitionAr: رِقّة في القلب تقتضي الإحسان والعطف

senses.definitionEn: The womb (raḥim); hence the tie of kinship and blood-relationship (ṣilat al-raḥim).
senses.definitionAr: الرَّحِم: موضع تكوين الولد، ومنه صلة الرحم

senses.definitionEn: raḥmān: an intense, all-embracing mercy (a form denoting fullness); raḥīm: mercy that is continual and enacted toward its objects.
senses.definitionAr: الرحمن: الواسع الرحمة، والرحيم: الدائم الإحسان
senses.notes: Distinction commonly drawn by the lexicographers.

senses.definitionEn: Praise; commendation of a person for excellence or beauty, offered willingly and joined with love and reverence.
senses.definitionAr: الثناء بالجميل على وجه التعظيم والمحبّة

senses.definitionEn: Broader than shukr (thankfulness for a benefit received): ḥamd may be for intrinsic excellence, not only for a favour.
maḥmūd: praiseworthy; one whose outcome and conduct are praised.
To worship, to serve with devotion; to turn to in reverent adoration.
To be bewildered or awe-struck; the mind's turning in wonder toward the divine.
ilāh: an object of worship; al-ilāh, contracted to 'Allāh' — the One truly deserving of worship. 'Allāh' functions as the proper name of God.
senses.definitionAr: الإله: المعبود، والله عَلَم على المعبود بحقّ

senses.definitionEn: To be high, elevated, lofty; to rise and be raised up.
samāʾ: the sky, heaven — that which is above and overarches.
ism (a name): the mark or sign by which a thing is known and brought to notice — derived from this root on one classical view.
senses.definitionAr: الاسم: ما دلّ على المسمّى وعُرف به
senses.notes: Others derive ism from و س م (a mark).

senses.definitionEn: To nurture and foster; to rear a thing stage by stage until it is complete.
senses.definitionAr: ربّى الشيء: أنشأه حالًا فحالًا إلى التمام

senses.definitionEn: rabb: lord, master, owner — one who has authority, ownership, and the care of a thing.
To gather, to have charge over, to possess and set in order.
To know; to apprehend a thing as it truly is, with certainty.
ʿalam: a mark, sign, or landmark by which a way or thing is known.
ʿālam: the world, the creation — all created things by which the Creator is known; ʿālamīn: all the worlds / all peoples.
senses.definitionAr: العالَم: الخلق كلّه، والعالمون: أصناف الخلق

senses.definitionEn: To possess, own, and have command over; to hold with authority to dispose of.
senses.definitionAr: حاز الشيء وقدر على التصرّف فيه

senses.definitionEn: mālik: an owner/possessor; malik: a king, sovereign; mulk: kingship and dominion.
senses.definitionAr: المالك: ذو الملك، والمَلِك: السلطان، والمُلك: العظمة والسلطان
senses.notes: Both readings Mālik and Malik occur at Q 1:4.

senses.definitionEn: Firmness and strength; to knead and strengthen a thing.
A day: the period from the rising of dawn to sunset; also a full day-and-night.
senses.definitionAr: اليوم: من طلوع الفجر إلى الغروب، أو مطلق الزمان

senses.definitionEn: A time, an epoch, or a momentous occasion or event.
yawm al-dīn: the Day of Judgement / Recompense.
Requital, recompense, judgement — 'as you deal, so you are dealt with' (kamā tadīnu tudānu).
Obedience, submission, and humble service.
Religion, way, custom, or law that one follows; also dayn: a debt or obligation.
senses.definitionAr: الدِّين: الملّة والعادة، والدَّين: القرض والالتزام
senses.notes: A notably wide range: judgement / obedience / religion / debt.

senses.definitionEn: To worship; to serve and obey with the utmost humility and submission.
ʿabd: a servant, bondsman, worshipper; one owned and devoted.
A road made smooth and level by treading (ṭarīq muʿabbad) — humbled and made even.
To help, aid, and assist; to support in a task.
ʿawn: help, aid, assistance; muʿīn: a helper.
istaʿāna (Form X): to seek help or assistance.
senses.notes: The verb at Q 1:5 (nastaʿīn) is Form X.

senses.definitionEn: To guide; to lead and direct rightly, to show the way with gentleness.
hudā: right guidance and direction; the opposite of straying.
hadiyya: a gift — that which is sent forward and directed to someone.
senses.definitionAr: الهديّة: ما يُوجَّه إلى الغير إكرامًا

senses.definitionEn: A road or way; especially a wide, clear, well-trodden path.
ṣirāṭ: the highway or open way that is travelled.
Related to sarṭ (to swallow): a road that 'swallows' those who travel it.
senses.definitionAr: من السَّرْط؛ كأنّ الطريق يبتلع سالكيه

senses.definitionEn: To stand, to rise up, to be established and erect.
To be straight and right; istaqāma (Form X): to be upright/straight; mustaqīm: straight, without crookedness.
senses.definitionAr: استقام: اعتدل، والمستقيم: ما لا اعوجاج فيه
senses.notes: mustaqīm at Q 1:6 is a Form-X active participle.

senses.definitionEn: qawm: a people (who rise together in an affair); qiyām: standing, subsistence.
To be soft, pleasant, and agreeable; ease, comfort, and a good manner of living.
niʿma: a favour, blessing, or bounty; anʿama (Form IV): to bestow a favour.
senses.definitionAr: النِّعمة: ما يُنعَم به، وأنعم: أفضل وأحسن
senses.notes: anʿamta at Q 1:7 is Form IV.

senses.definitionEn: naʿam: 'yes', an expression of assent; also al-naʿam: pasturing cattle as bestowed wealth.
senses.definitionAr: نَعَمْ: حرف تصديق، والنَّعَم: الإبل والماشية

senses.definitionEn: Otherness, difference; ghayr: other than, different from — used also for exception or negation ('not').
senses.definitionAr: الاختلاف؛ غَيْر: سوى وخلاف، وتفيد الاستثناء والنفي

senses.definitionEn: To change or alter a thing (ghayyara).
ghayra: protective jealousy, zeal for what one holds dear.
Anger, wrath; the opposite of riḍā (good pleasure).
To be angry with someone; maghḍūb: one against whom anger is directed.
senses.definitionAr: غضب عليه، والمغضوب: من وقع عليه الغضب
senses.notes: maghḍūb at Q 1:7 is a passive participle.

senses.definitionEn: Intensity and severity; a reddening (as of the face in anger); ghaḍūb: quick to anger.
To go astray; to lose or miss the way; to err.
To be lost, to vanish or perish; ḍalāl: error, straying, being lost (opposite of guidance).
ḍāll: one who strays or is misguided; also confusion and perplexity.
senses.notes: ḍāllīn at Q 1:7 is the sound-plural active participle.
